title: staff — subprocessors
description: The Staff module itself engages ZERO third-party subprocessors — every state transition happens within the Stackra platform. The Notifications module dispatches staff lifecycle notifications via its own subprocessors (mail, SMS, push); those are declared in notifications::subprocessors.json. The Finance module (Wave 4) will engage payroll processors that consume compensation_amount_cents; those live in finance::subprocessors.json. This file lists ZERO active subprocessors to be explicit about the module's boundary — staff state is fully first-party.

downstream_subprocessors_referenced.used_for: Dispatching StaffWelcome / StaffOffboarded / StaffPromoted / CoachCertificationExpiring / CompensationChanged / ProbationEndingSoon / StaffSuspended notifications via mail / SMS / push channels.

Payroll integration — 3rd-party payroll processors (Gusto / ADP / Deel / regional equivalents) consume compensation_amount_cents + pay_frequency for the tenant's chosen payroll flow. Those subprocessors own the full-tax-ID storage that we deliberately don't.
Signed-document uploads for coach certification verification (when coach_certifications_verified entitlement is on). Storage module engages S3-compatible object storage subprocessors.

notes: This file exists deliberately even with an empty subprocessors array. The GDPR Art. 30 record of processing activities requires per-module subprocessor lists; an empty list is the correct declaration for a module that engages none directly.

If a future Wave introduces a subprocessor into this module (e.g. an AI-based org-chart suggester or a certification-verification service that scans uploaded documents), add it here + update compliance.json Art. 28 evidence + refresh the tenant-facing subprocessor list.
